dr_section_ref: DR §1 — Indonesian discourse particles (kok, sentence-final use)
notes: Sentence-final 'kok' admitting a state ('belum mandi' = 'haven't showered') with mild self-defense or honest-disclosure register — the partner pressed and the speaker is conceding. Note: 'belum' translates as 'not yet' / 'haven't yet' (perfect-negative); the English target includes 'yet' to preserve the aspectual meaning. VERIFY WITH GIRLFRIEND: 'I haven't showered, I swear' might fit better when the partner is teasing; 'honestly' is the more neutral-conceding version.
